evals.prompt:
Streaming ASR system using Qwen3-ASR model on Apple Silicon + MLX.

Architecture: audio is split into fixed 2-second chunks. Each chunk is independently fed to the model for decoding (multi-turn KV cache, each chunk = one user turn).

Problem 1: A 5-minute Japanese business conversation produces 106 occurrences of "はい" in the transcript, far more than actually spoken. Full-file (non-streaming) transcription does not have this issue.

Problem 2: Speech that spans chunk boundaries gets transcribed twice. For example, "体験版プレイしたから" crosses two chunks and outputs "プレイしたから。プレイしたから。"

Reference implementation: /Users/zsshao/dev/mlx-qwen3-asr/mlx_qwen3_asr/streaming.py — read this file first before starting analysis.

What is the best way to fix these issues?
evals.expected_output: Root cause: fixed-size chunking is speech-unaware — it sends silent segments to the model (causing hallucination) and splits mid-utterance speech (causing duplication). The recommended eliminate-category solution is to integrate a Voice Activity Detection (VAD) model like Silero VAD to make chunking speech-aware: only send chunks containing actual speech, and cut at speech boundaries rather than arbitrary 2-second intervals.
expectations: Root cause identifies that fixed 2-second chunking is fundamentally speech-unaware
Problem 1 (hallucinated はい) is traced to the model receiving silent/near-silent chunks and generating filler
Problem 2 (cross-boundary duplication) is traced to speech being split mid-utterance by fixed boundaries
Both problems are recognized as sharing the same root cause (speech-unaware chunking)
VAD (Voice Activity Detection) is proposed as a solution — specifically Silero VAD or equivalent
The VAD solution is categorized as 'eliminate' (removes the root cause of speech-unaware chunking)
The existing energy-based endpointing in the reference code is identified as insufficient compared to a proper VAD model
GitHub or web search is performed to validate the VAD approach
The analysis references the streaming.py file and identifies relevant code (e.g., _select_decode_samples, energy endpointing)
